title: Lambda-backed defer macros show defer semantics, not wrapper ceremony
summary: DANS_DEFER and DEFER hide their macro call, capture list, and lambda wrapper; a single statement displays as defer statement and a multi-statement body displays as a defer block.
details: DEFER([&] { free(Z); }); displays as defer free(Z);.
DANS_DEFER and DEFER are equivalent recognized spellings rather than relying on a concealed DANS_ prefix to produce accidental support.
A one-line body containing multiple statements retains braces so statement grouping stays visible.
A multiline opener becomes defer { and its Tree-sitter-confirmed closing }); becomes a bare closing brace; body rows remain on their original displayed rows.
Cursor and Visual reveal show the complete source macro and lambda wrapper.
rationale: The macro exists to express deferred execution; its lambda capture machinery is implementation ceremony in the frontend view.
Keeping braces for multiple statements avoids changing the perceived scope of deferred work.
alternatives: Only hide the DANS_ prefix and leave DEFER([&] { ... }); visible; rejected because it exposes the implementation rather than the semantic construct.
Flatten every body without braces; rejected because a multi-statement defer would become visually ambiguous.

provenance.note: Owner required a single-line defer wrapper to show only the deferred semantic body rather than lambda ceremony.
Structured single-line parsing and Tree-sitter-confirmed multiline closure implement both macro names.

evidence.reference: tests/frontend_spec.lua plain and DANS defer cases
evidence.claim: Single-statement, multi-statement, and multiline forms for both spellings are pinned.

evidence.reference: style_lab/catalog.json cuda-control-flow scene and real ext-linegrid capture
evidence.claim: Actual Neovim output shows defer free(Z); with the defer keyword highlighted as a language construct.

questions: Why are defer lambda captures hidden?
What happens to multiline defer source rows?

last_verified.method: Owner instruction verified by focused overlay cases and actual ext-linegrid capture.
